data.title: Elon Musk's Grokipedia Nears Wikipedia's Scale in Just Months
data.description:
Elon Musk's Grokipedia has grown quickly since its launch and now contains 5,615,201 articles, which is nearly 79% of the size of English Wikipedia's 7,119,376 entries. The AI-powered encyclopedia was launched by Musk's xAI just a few months ago and has already become one of the largest knowledge bases on the internet. At its current pace, many observers believe that it is only a matter of time before Grokipedia surpasses Wikipedia in total article count.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

From the start, xAI has said that Grokipedia is designed to be more neutral and less biased than traditional, human-edited encyclopedias by using Grok's reasoning abilities to quickly draft, check, and update information. Musk also described it as an important step toward xAI's larger goal of better understanding the universe. When Grokipedia launched in late October as version 0.1, it drew a lot of praise, including from Wikipedia co-founder Larry Sanger, who highlighted how AI could help fill knowledge gaps and keep articles more current.

Since then, Grokipedia has been updated to version 0.2 and continues to grow, even though it is still text-only for now. Musk has also shared much bigger plans for the platform, including eventually renaming it Encyclopedia Galactica. He says the goal is to turn it into an open-source collection of all human knowledge, including audio, images, and video. Musk has even talked about sending copies of it into space, so humanity's knowledge is never lost again.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 9.3% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Fed's Bostic Says 'Inflation is Still Too High'
data.description:
The core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index, which is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation, rose by 2.4% in the latest reading, which is still above the central bank's target of 2%. That's a big problem, said Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic on Friday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Inflation is still too high," said Bostic in an interview with the WLRN radio station in Florida. Bostic added that the Fed must be "laser-focused on making sure that everything we do is contributing" to lower inflation.

Fed Expected to Hold Rates Despite Miran's Call for Cuts

Lower interest rates can contribute to rising inflation by making borrowing cheaper, which encourages spending and investment and puts upward pressure on prices. Investors seem to agree, with CME's FedWatch tool showing a 95% chance that the Fed holds rates steady during its first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of the year on January 28.

On the other side of the argument is Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran, who said earlier this week that inflation is "within noise" of the Fed's target and that lower rates are necessary to support a softening labor market. Miran added that he supports 150 bps of rate cuts by year-end.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 01/09/2026
data.description:
If you're aiming for dependable cash flow from your portfolio in 2026, dividend aristocrat stocks are a rich hunting ground for durable, income‑focused opportunities

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock has a dividend payout ratio of at least 40%, meaning that each company distributes at least 40% of its net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with at least 10% upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. S&P Global (SPGI)— S&P Global is a prominent American provider of financial data analytics and market intelligence, credit ratings, and benchmarks, among others. The New York-based company's dividend payout ratio is currently 44.39%. S&P Global paid 96 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 0.71%. SPGI stock presently holds a Strong Buy consensus rating from 15 Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $617.77, implying about 14% upside potential.
  2. PPG Industries (PPG) — PPG Industries is a U.S.-based company focused on paints, coatings, and specialty materials, including sealants and transparent armor used in commercial and military aircraft applications. The Pennsylvania-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 72.39%. The firm paid 71 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 2.68%. PPG stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus from 13 analysts on Wall Street, with an average price target of $116.92 that suggests approximately 10% upside.
  3. Walmart (WMT) — Walmart is one of America's biggest retail giants. It operates a vast network of stores that includes hypermarkets, discount department stores, and grocery outlets. The company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 88.85%. It paid 23 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 0.83%. WMT stock currently holds a Strong Buy consensus rating from 14 Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $125.75, implying approximately 10% upside potential.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Companies known as dividend aristocrats sit within the S&P 500 index (SPX) and have not only paid dividends for many years but have also increased them annually for at least 25 consecutive years.

Because of this lengthy, rising payout record, investors often favor these stocks when they want steady cash returns rather than volatile share prices. A detail that can easily be missed is that newly listed spin‑offs may also qualify as dividend aristocrats, since they are considered to inherit their former parent's long streak of dividend growth.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

To find more stocks like these, take a look at TipRanks' Dividend Aristocrats tool.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.title: Airbnb's (ABNB) Senior Executives Sell Millions Worth of Company Stock
data.description:
Airbnb (ABNB) saw a wave of selling among its senior executive ranks in December.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company's chief executive officer (CEO), chief financial officer (CFO), chief technology officer, and co-founder Nathan Blecharczyk each sold ABNB stock heading into the end of 2025. CEO Brian Cheskey alone sold 60,000 shares of the company for proceeds of $7.86 million.

Airbnb's CTO Aristotle Balogh sold more than $2 million of company stock, while CFO Elinor Mertz also sold about $2 million worth of ABNB shares she owned. However, it was Airbnb co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer Nathan Blecharczyk who was most aggressive, selling more than $20 million of company stock.

Why The ABNB Stock Sales?

It's not clear why so many senior executives at homestay and vacation rental company Airbnb sold stock in December. The sales came after Airbnb reported mixed financial results for last year's third quarter. The company announced earnings per share (EPS) of $2.21, which was below the $2.34 expected among analysts.

However, revenue in the July through August period totaled $4.10 billion, which was slightly ahead of the $4.08 billion anticipated on Wall Street. Sales were up 10% from a year ago. Management highlighted new features that include improved maps, updated cancellation policies, and a reserve now, pay later option for customers.

Is ABNB Stock a Buy?

Airbnb stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 28 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, 16 Hold and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ABNB price target of $143.53 implies 2.60% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Household Net Worth Ramps to Record High amid S&P 500 Rally
data.description:
Americans are wealthier than ever, with household net worth rising by $6.1 trillion to $181.6 trillion during the third quarter, according to a report published by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A rally in the S&P 500 (SPX) helped bolster that figure, with household equity holdings rising by $5.5 trillion during the quarter. The benchmark index returned 17.9% in 2025, marking its third consecutive year of double-digit gains.

Household Debt Rises Alongside Weak Consumer Sentiment

At the same time, consumer borrowing increased at an annualized pace of 4.1%, marking the highest rate of growth since 2022. This comes amid falling median housing prices and mortgage rates above 6%, with household real estate holdings falling by $287 billion.

Furthermore, consumer sentiment remains subdued as Americans worry about higher prices and a softening labor market. The University of Michigan's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment for January was 54, rising from 52.9 in December but still down by 24.7% year-over-year.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Walmart is Watching You: New AI Tool to Analyze Audience Behavior Gets Mexican Launch
data.description:
Walmart (WMT) Connect Mexico has launched AI in-store audience intelligence sensors to better analyze the behavior of customers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Real Time Analysis

Walmart Connect Mexico is the omnichannel retail media business of Walmart in Mexico and Central America. The rollout will reportedly take place in hundreds of Walmart Supercenters, with thousands of sensors measuring and activating in-store retail media campaigns in real-time.

In partnership with AI firm Advertime, the sensors will analyze shoppers to gain a better understanding of their age group, gender, attention span and dwell time.

Walmart Connect Mexico said the focus of the technology rollout will be on audience measurement in non-digital promotional spaces within stores; digital media within stores, including digital signage; and real-time audience targeting.

The information from the sensors will be fed into Walmart Connect's multimedia platform, with the aim of developing more accurate digital and static media campaigns.

Wei Pung, global head of market strategy and growth at Advertima, said: "For the first time at hypermarket scale, real-time audience measurement and audience-based activation are becoming operational inside the physical store."

Walmart's AI Growth

It is a further example of Walmart's transformation into a technology and retail business.

Over the last 12 months, Walmart has embraced the AI and technological revolution. This includes developing its GenAI chatbot Sparky, which helps customers plan their purchases, and a partnership with OpenAI.

That deal aims to bring AI into more parts of its retail experience. Now, customers can shop at Walmart through ChatGPT by using a new feature called Instant Checkout. It has also moved its listing from the New York Stock Exchange to the more tech-heavy Nasdaq.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 'Structural Winner of the AI Wars': Scotiabank Crowns Alphabet Stock (GOOGL)
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOGL) shares edged higher on Friday as Scotiabank proclaimed the U.S. tech giant the "structural winner of the artificial intelligence wars" among hyperscalers despite its relatively slower entry.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Scotiabank analyst Nat Schindler reaffirmed his Buy rating on Alphabet's shares and raised his price target by about 12% from $336 per share to $375, implying about 15% upside from the stock's closing price on Thursday. With gains of almost 65%, Alphabet's shares led the "Magnificent Seven" group in 2025 in terms of stock performance.

In giving his rating, Schindler emphasized the fact that GOOGL stock has climbed about 64% over the past 12 months, adding that "every decision they make [at Google] seems to be the correct one."

Scotiabank Bullish on Google Cloud

In addition, the analyst pointed to Alphabet's Wall Street-beating third-quarter earnings results that were "very strong across the board," including Search, YouTube and Cloud.

"We're particularly bullish on the potential upside within Cloud, especially on operating margin," Schindler noted, adding that he expects the tech giant's stock to outperform due to its positioning to benefit from the monetization of AI. The analyst backed his bullish view despite acknowledging that there may be "some noise" from non‑cash charges related to Google's capital assets, data centers, and servers, etc. — that is, depreciation and amortization.

Mizuho Sees Alphabet's Waymo Scaling in 2026

Meanwhile, Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley in his latest assessment of the internet sector, noted that he expects the "AI megatrend" seen in 2025 to continue this year. Walmsley anticipates focus to shift to product changes and ad monetization by OpenAI and Alphabet's scaling of Waymo, the self-driving unit that reported industry-leading 14 million rides between January and early December 2025.

The five-star analyst also reaffirmed his Buy rating on Google and raised his GOOGL price target by 12% to $365, implying about 11% upside.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Alphabet's shares continue to boast a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts based on 27 Buys and six Holds issued over the past three months by 33 analysts.

However, the average GOOGL price target of $337.87 implies less than 3% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Trump Tariff Case Won't Receive a Supreme Court Ruling on Friday
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court won't issue a ruling on the legality of President Trump's tariffs today, according to Reuters. Earlier this week, the nation's highest court announced that it would meet for opinion day at 10 a.m. on Friday. SCOTUS doesn't provide advance notice of which decisions it will release, although investors speculated that the case could receive expedited treatment due to its significance. The next opinion day has been scheduled for Wednesday, January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The court heard oral arguments for the case last November, with some justices expressing skepticism that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) provides legal authority for the tariffs. The IEEPA allows the president to enforce tariffs in response to national emergencies.

Trump Team Eyes Backup Tariff Plans

Earlier today, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said the Trump administration is considering other legal avenues to impose tariffs if the court issues an unfavorable outcome. "Our expectation is that ⁠we're going to ​win, and if we don't win, then we know that we've got other tools ​that we could use that get us to the same place," Hassett told CNBC.

Potential backup plans include Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act, which allow the president to impose tariffs or trade restrictions to address unfair trade practices or protect domestic industries.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "The Math Doesn't Add Up Anymore": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Gains, the Berlin Gigafactory at Risk?
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) has been having a few issues of late, as its status as the dominant figure in electric vehicles is increasingly under fire. Not only are there more competitors than ever, but there are more competitors pursuing slices of a dwindling pie. Some are starting to wonder if the Berlin Gigafactory is now at risk as a result of all these market changes. The idea that it may be on the chopping block caught interest, though, and sent shares up over 2% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The idea behind a Berlin Gigafactory was worthwhile enough. Europe was a major market for electric cars, so why not have a factory there that builds them? Lower shipping costs, few if any tariff issues, and several other benefits made the notion a good idea.

But, some look at the picture now and believe that "the math doesn't add up anymore." After all, the idea of having a factory in a location to sell cars really only works if you are selling cars in sufficient quantity to make a factory worthwhile. Tesla is currently selling fewer vehicles in Europe than it did before the Berlin Gigafactory went online. With that combination in mind, it is easy to wonder how much use there even is for the Berlin Gigafactory any more.

A New Supplier, Sort Of

Meanwhile, we got a look at how some of Elon Musk's less-visible businesses are operating, and one exciting point came to light. Musk's xAI revealed that it was going to start supplying AI to the Tesla Optimus project. This does, somewhat, damage the notion that Tesla is an AI company, because Tesla will now have little to do with AI itself. Rather, it will get its AI from a separate source.

While xAI's losses seem to be growing, and the company burns through cash at a staggering rate, the idea remains clear: xAI wants to "…develop self-sufficient AI to power robots like Tesla's Optimus." So what remains for Tesla, if xAI will be handling the AI part of the Optimus robot? Without the AI, Tesla is little more than a "contract manufacturer" for xAI.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.4% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 9.4% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why SanDisk Stock (SNDK) Is Surging Today
data.description:
Shares of chipmaker SanDisk (SNDK) are surging today after Nomura Securities said that the firm plans to sharply raise the prices of its high-capacity 3D NAND memory chips. Indeed, Nomura says that SanDisk's prices could more than double this quarter as demand for storage in AI servers continues to surge. While it is not yet clear how much this will affect the flash memory used in smartphones and laptops, those chips are often made in the same factories, so price increases in enterprise storage usually spread to consumer devices over time.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nomura's research shows that memory suppliers across the industry are already raising prices, with enterprise-grade NAND seeing the biggest increases. The analysts believe this is happening because of both short-term shortages and long-term growth in demand, driven mainly by artificial intelligence systems that require massive amounts of fast, reliable storage. One of the biggest drivers of this demand is Nvidia's (NVDA) AI infrastructure, especially its new Inference Context Memory Storage Platform.

Each Nvidia VR NVL144 rack uses 18 special data-processing units, and each one includes a 512-gigabyte SSD, which adds up to over 9 terabytes of NAND memory per rack. If Nvidia ships 50,000 of these racks a year, it would need nearly half an exabyte of NAND just for this one product line. Even though the global industry produces hundreds of exabytes each year, the fast growth of AI systems is pushing demand closer to supply limits, which is why prices are now rising so quickly.

Is SNDK Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on SNDK stock based on 36 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SNDK price target of $283.69 per share implies 22.3% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
